message.content:
Run CODEX (GPT-5.6) to gate a batch of low-risk code renames. Write /tmp/rename_gate.md then run 'cd /tmp && timeout 240 codex exec --skip-git-repo-check - < /tmp/rename_gate.md 2>&1 | tail -30' (bubblewrap warning fine; embed evidence inline; medium effort; no sandbox flags).
Context: We executed TEXT-ONLY + SAFE-static renames from a codex-pre-validated worklist. Rename result: {"text_applied":9,"safe_applied":86,"skipped":["_run_honest_sweep_r3 (iwire014_quantified_replay.py:63) — TEXT-ONLY row but the token does not exist in the file; only references to the MODULE filename run_honest_sweep_r3.py exist (lines 31/53/59). No valid rename target; renaming the module file is out of scope.","build_and_run_v4 -> build_and_run (live_server.py:557) AND build_and_run_v4 -> build_and_run_pipeline_a_ui (pipeline_a_ui_adapter.py:187) — SKIPPED as one linked cluster. It is a LIVE import (not a docstring) and the symbol is asserted by a source-grep STRING in tests/polaris_graph/test_b102_graph_v4.py:197 ('pipeline_a_ui_adapter import build_and_run_v4' in source). Dynamic/source-inspection reference => NEEDS-ALIAS, not safe.","POLARIS BEAT-BOTH -> POLARIS Benchmark Report (report_renderer.py:126) — labeled SAFE-static const but the token lives inside an HTML <title> STRING LITERAL, not a code symbol. Rule forbids editing string literals for SAFE-static rows; belongs in TEXT-ONLY class.","HonestSweepJobRunner / HonestSweepJobRunnerConfig / make_default_honest_sweep_job_runner (audit_ir/__init__.py:29-32 and honest_sweep_job_runner.py:177/450) — SKIPPED as a cluster. Worklist gives CONFLICTING targets: __init__ re-export maps to SweepJobRunner but the definition maps to V30SweepJobRunner (and make_default to make_default_sweep_job_runner vs make_default_v30_sweep_job_runner). A re-export must match its definition; the two rows disagree. 15 references across a public API — needs human reconciliation of the single canonical name before applying.","_screen_junk_evidence (run_honest_sweep_r3.py:1248 and :14016) — externally referenced by a source-grep STRING harness: scripts/iarch_beatboth011_junk_source_screen_harness.py lines 468-470 assert 'def _screen_junk_evidence(' in src and src.count('_screen_junk_evidence('). Renaming breaks the harness's textual assertions. NEEDS-ALIAS.","_detect_ci_junk (run_honest_sweep_r3.py:14655) — the cited line is a comment block, not a definition. The actual _detect_ci_junk is only a LOCAL import ALIAS (line 15655: 'import detect_content_integrity_junk as _detect_ci_junk'); the real symbol is detect_content_integrity_junk defined in src/polaris_graph/retrieval/. Location/target mismatch and the real rename is a larger cross-module change — needs human review.","_PAID_PATH_WINNER_FLAGS (run_honest_sweep_r3.py:21675) — referenced as a STRING for dynamic namespace lookup in tests/polaris_graph/test_wave_b_ws2_paid_slate.py (ns['_PAID_PATH_WINNER_FLAGS'], and listed in a _WANTED string set). Dynamic reference => NEEDS-ALIAS.","_ARTIFACT_KIND_HEADINGS (run_honest_sweep_r3.py:6700) — no-op row (old_name == new_name) with a malformed SUGGESTED field ('Declined — no report produced'). No actionable rename; needs human review (as the worklist note flagged).","OpenAIShimClient (scripts/_retired_2026_06_14/pg_compose_openai_validation.py:58) — VERDICT=RENAME but SUGGESTED target is '-' (no target). In a retired directory covered by a separate dir-retirement issue. No rename target.","dice -> StageCheckResult (pipeline_diced_preflight.py:217) — no class literally named 'dice' at that location; the nearby class is DiceResult and line 217 is a comment ('Dice result + context'). 'dice' is also used pervasively as the English domain word across src/ comments/docstrings. Ambiguous target; a real rename would be DiceResult->StageCheckResult (bigger than specified). Needs human review.","contracts_v3 -> contracts (analysis_notebook.py:14) — 'contracts_v3' here is a MODULE name ('from src.polaris_graph.contracts_v3 import AnalysisEntry'), referenced across ~20 files (nodes/, tests/v3/). Target 'contracts' is generic (worklist itself hedged: 'or descriptive submodule name') and would require a module-file rename with collision risk. Too broad/ambiguous to apply safely.","is_junk -> is_chrome_screen (weighted_enrichment.py:5212) — 'is_junk' is reused as a local var / function parameter across MULTIPLE overlapping scopes in the same file (lines 4860 param, 4911, 5213, 5245, 5261, 5349, 5379, 5396) and also appears as an OBJECT ATTRIBUTE (getattr(verdict,'is_junk',...)) in run_honest_sweep_r3.py. Cannot line-scope a single-var rename safely with word-boundary replace without corrupting the other scopes/attribute. Needs human review.","_m2_dt -> _stamp_document_genre (live_retriever.py:7261) — module-level function patched by STRING name via monkeypatch.setattr(lr, '_m2_dt', ...) in tests/polaris_graph/test_wave2_fetch_throughput.py (3 sites). Dynamic reference => NEEDS-ALIAS.","_w5_tier_batch_idx -> _tier_batch_index (live_retriever.py:7774) — used as a STRING DICT KEY that flows through data structures ('_row["_w5_tier_batch_idx"]', '_ev_row.pop("_w5_tier_batch_idx")') and is source-checked in scripts/iwire001_w5w2_unboundlocal_integration_fire_test.py ('_w5_tier_batch_idx' in seed_rows[0]). String data-contract => NEEDS-ALIAS. (Its sibling _w5_loop_idx, the value assigned, WAS renamed.)","rhsr_patched -> honest_sweep_module (harness_render_boundary_screen.py:57) — exists ONLY as a STRING literal (synthetic module name passed to importlib.util.spec_from_file_location and registered in sys.modules['rhsr_patched']), never as an identifier. Rule forbids editing string literals for SAFE-static rows. NEEDS-ALIAS/TEXT-ONLY reclass.","rhsr3_canary -> sweep_module_canary (iarch007_behavioral_canary.py:157) — exists ONLY as a STRING literal (module name arg to importlib.util.spec_from_file_location), not an identifier. Same reason as rhsr_patched."],"collection_after":"16738 tests collected, 11 errors (unchanged from the green baseline; all 76 modified .py files py_compile cleanly). The 11 collection errors are the same pre-existing environment/dependency failures present before this work. Separately verified: the 3 runtime failures in tests/polaris_graph/blockers/test_g2_runsweep_blockers.py (test_iarch011_prb_*) FAIL identically on the clean stashed baseline, so they are pre-existing and NOT caused by any rename."}. Validation: {"collection_ok":true,"characterization_ok":true,"oracle_sha":"9c0a3d438da943242c98e2fe714494c342d42d02102202d75a61a4554339db98","oracle_matches":true,"notes":"All three gates PASS. The renames in /home/polaris/wt/phase2 (branch chore/review-readiness-phase2) are score-safe.

GATE 1 — Full test COLLECTION: PASS (matches baseline floor exactly).
  Result: "16738 tests collected, 11 errors in 11.44s"
  Baseline: collected=16738, errors=11. Both match. No regression from the rename work; the 11 errors are the known pre-existing environment/dependency collection failures.

GATE 2 — Config characterization: PASS.
  Command: pytest tests/test_config_registry.py tests/test_settings_models.py -q
  Result: "9 passed in 0.11s" — still green.

GATE 3 — ORACLE replay on covered outline path: PASS.
  Command: PG_OUTLINE_AGENT_MAX_TURNS=3 ... tests/oracle/acceptance_portable.py --replay
  Artifact SHA-256: 9c0a3d438da943242c98e2fe714494c342d42d02102202d75a61a4554339db98 — EQUALS the golden. Replay artifact is BYTE-IDENTICAL to recorded golden (3184 bytes).
  Both controls pass: negative control valid (valid_negative_control=true) and positive/full loop valid (full_loop_ran=true, finish_outline ACCEPTED at turn 1). Final line: "ACCEPTANCE PASSED: all run controls valid".

The oracle files did NOT need the same rename — the covered outline path replayed byte-identical, so the oracle still references consistent symbols and behavior did not move. No oracle-path rename inconsistency observed."}. The renames were required to keep pytest --collect-only at baseline error count and to keep the deterministic oracle replay byte-identical (golden 9c0a3d438da943242c98e2fe714494c342d42d02102202d75a61a4554339db98) on the covered path.
Ask codex: "Given test-collection is unchanged from baseline, config characterization passes, and the oracle replay is still byte-identical, are these SAFE-static + TEXT-ONLY renames reference-complete and score-safe to commit? Any residual risk (a symbol renamed but a dynamic/string reference missed)? End with: RENAMES-SAFE or RENAMES-REVISE."
Return codex's verdict + points.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll write the gate prompt and run codex to gate these renames.

input.content:
You are gating a batch of low-risk code renames for score-safety before commit.

## What was done
We executed TEXT-ONLY + SAFE-static renames from a codex-pre-validated worklist in a Python monorepo (branch chore/review-readiness-phase2). Rename classes:
- TEXT-ONLY: identifiers in docstrings/comments only.
- SAFE-static: static code symbols (functions/classes/consts) with NO dynamic/string/source-grep references, renamed with word-boundary replace across all static references.

## Rename result
- text_applied: 9
- safe_applied: 86
- 17 rows SKIPPED (deferred to human), each for a concrete reason. Representative skip reasons:
  - Token does not exist in file (only the module filename does) — no valid target.
  - build_and_run_v4: LIVE import AND asserted by a source-grep STRING in tests (test_b102_graph_v4.py:197 checks 'pipeline_a_ui_adapter import build_and_run_v4' in source) => NEEDS-ALIAS.
  - "POLARIS BEAT-BOTH": token lives inside an HTML <title> string literal, not a code symbol => reclass to TEXT-ONLY.
  - HonestSweepJobRunner cluster: worklist gives CONFLICTING targets ( __init__ re-export vs definition disagree) across 15 refs of a public API => human reconcile.
  - _screen_junk_evidence: source-grep STRING harness asserts 'def _screen_junk_evidence(' in src and src.count('_screen_junk_evidence(') => NEEDS-ALIAS.
  - _detect_ci_junk: cited line is a comment; real symbol is a local import alias of detect_content_integrity_junk => location/target mismatch.
  - _PAID_PATH_WINNER_FLAGS: referenced as STRING for dynamic namespace lookup in tests (ns['_PAID_PATH_WINNER_FLAGS']) => NEEDS-ALIAS.
  - _ARTIFACT_KIND_HEADINGS: no-op row (old==new) with malformed suggested field => human review.
  - OpenAIShimClient: VERDICT=RENAME but target is '-' , in a retired dir => no target.
  - dice -> StageCheckResult: no class literally named 'dice'; nearby class is DiceResult; 'dice' is pervasive English domain word => ambiguous.
  - contracts_v3 -> contracts: MODULE name referenced across ~20 files; generic target; module-file rename collision risk => too broad.
  - is_junk -> is_chrome_screen: reused as local var/param across multiple overlapping scopes AND as object attribute getattr(verdict,'is_junk',...) => cannot line-scope safely.
  - _m2_dt: module-level function patched by STRING name via monkeypatch.setattr(lr,'_m2_dt',...) in tests (3 sites) => NEEDS-ALIAS.
  - _w5_tier_batch_idx: used as STRING DICT KEY flowing through data structures and source-checked in a script => string data-contract => NEEDS-ALIAS.
  - rhsr_patched / rhsr3_canary: exist ONLY as STRING literals (module name arg to importlib.util.spec_from_file_location / sys.modules), never as identifiers => NEEDS-ALIAS/TEXT-ONLY reclass.

Note: every skip is either (a) a dynamic/string/source-grep reference, (b) a conflicting/ambiguous/missing target, or (c) a string-literal that violates the SAFE-static rule. The applied set explicitly excluded all of these.

## Validation gates (all PASS)
- GATE 1 — Full test COLLECTION: pytest --collect-only => "16738 tests collected, 11 errors" — EXACTLY matches the green baseline (16738/11). The 11 errors are pre-existing environment/dependency collection failures present before this work. All 76 modified .py files py_compile cleanly.
- GATE 2 — Config characterization: pytest tests/test_config_registry.py tests/test_settings_models.py -q => "9 passed". Still green.
- GATE 3 — Deterministic ORACLE replay on covered outline path: artifact SHA-256 = 9c0a3d438da943242c98e2fe714494c342d42d02102202d75a61a4554339db98 EQUALS the golden (byte-identical, 3184 bytes). Both controls pass (valid_negative_control=true, full_loop_ran=true, finish_outline ACCEPTED at turn 1). Final: "ACCEPTANCE PASSED: all run controls valid".
- Separately verified: 3 runtime failures in test_g2_runsweep_blockers.py FAIL identically on the clean stashed baseline => pre-existing, NOT caused by renames.

## Your question
Given test-collection is unchanged from baseline, config characterization passes, and the oracle replay is still byte-identical, are these SAFE-static + TEXT-ONLY renames reference-complete and score-safe to commit? Any residual risk (a symbol renamed but a dynamic/string reference missed)? 

Reason at medium effort. Consider specifically:
1. Does pytest --collect-only actually import all modules such that a missed static reference would surface as a collection error? What does it NOT cover (lazy imports, runtime-only paths, string-based references)?
2. Given the skip discipline excluded all known dynamic/string/source-grep references, what residual class of missed reference could still exist that these three gates would NOT catch?
3. Is the byte-identical oracle replay meaningful evidence, or is it narrow to one covered path?

End your answer with exactly one token on its own line: RENAMES-SAFE or RENAMES-REVISE.

8	Yes—this is reasonable to commit as a low-risk rename batch, though “reference-complete” should be understood as repository-static completeness, not proof against every possible runtime or external reference.
9	
10	1. `pytest --collect-only` is useful but incomplete. It imports test modules and whatever application modules they import during collection, so it catches broken module-level imports, decorators, base classes, and other import-time lookups. It does not execute test bodies, fixtures, most function bodies, lazy imports, environment-gated branches, subprocess entry points, or optional integrations. Even an imported module can contain a stale name inside an unexecuted function without collection noticing. `py_compile` likewise validates syntax, not name resolution.

15	
16	   - Constructed strings, such as `"old_" + "name"`.
17	   - Reflection through `getattr`, `globals`, registries, import hooks, or configuration files.
18	   - Contracts derived implicitly from `__name__` or qualified names: CLI commands, routes, plugin registrations, metrics, schemas, or serialized/pickled objects.
19	   - Generated files, non-Python assets, ignored files, or downstream consumers outside the monorepo.

It demonstrates no observable artifact change along that execution path and its transitive dependencies. It does not establish equivalence for other commands, lazy branches, integrations, or public API consumers.

Residual risk is nonzero but confined mainly to undiscovered dynamic, name-derived, external, or unexecuted contracts—not ordinary in-repository static references.

content.text: Codex returned its verdict. I have the full output.

codex_points: Reasonable to commit as a low-risk rename batch, but 'reference-complete' should be read as repository-STATIC completeness, not proof against every runtime/external reference.

pytest --collect-only is useful but incomplete: it imports test + application modules (catching broken module-level imports, decorators, base classes, import-time lookups) but does NOT execute test bodies, fixtures, most function bodies, lazy imports, env-gated branches, subprocess entry points, or optional integrations. An imported module can still hold a stale name inside an unexecuted function without collection noticing. py_compile validates syntax, not name resolution.
Baseline comparison would be STRONGER if the collected node IDs and the identities of the 11 errors match, rather than merely their counts (recommended hardening, not a blocker).

For repo-wide whole-token replacement, ordinary Python references should be complete: a static identifier cannot hide from a correctly-scoped token search. Residual risk is confined to references OUTSIDE that model: constructed strings ('old_'+'name'); reflection via getattr/globals/registries/import hooks/config files; contracts derived from __name__ or qualified names (CLI commands, routes, plugin registrations, metrics, schemas, pickled/serialized objects); generated files, non-Python assets, ignored files, downstream consumers outside the monorepo; runtime-only branches not exercised by selected tests; docstrings consumed by doctest/source snapshots/runtime introspection; accidental over-renaming of an unrelated same-spelling identifier in another scope.
The skip discipline directly addresses the most likely instances of these hazards (dynamic/string/source-grep references were all deferred).
Recommended completeness check: a final RAW SEARCH of every applied OLD token across all tracked file types, with every remaining occurrence classified — this is more direct than collection alone.

Byte-identical oracle replay is STRONG evidence for the covered outline path; valid controls confirm the equality came from a genuine completed run, not a shortcut/invalid replay. It shows no observable artifact change along that execution path and its transitive deps, but does NOT establish equivalence for other commands, lazy branches, integrations, or public-API consumers (narrow to one path).

Overall: evidence supports committing — known dynamic/ambiguous cases excluded, static refs mechanically updated, collection did not regress, characterization green, relevant oracle path identical. Residual risk is nonzero but confined to undiscovered dynamic/name-derived/external/unexecuted contracts, NOT ordinary in-repo static references.
Codex final token: RENAMES-SAFE. Tokens used: 8094.
